message:
fix(channel): prevent wake-under-lock deadlock in mpsc sender cascade

Rename wake_next_sender() to take_next_sender_waker() which returns an
owned Waker instead of calling wake_by_ref() while holding the channel
lock. The caller now invokes waker.wake() after dropping the lock,
eliminating a potential deadlock when the woken task's poll re-acquires
the same channel lock.

Also handles the edge case where all capacity is consumed by reserved
slots with nothing to evict: send_with_eviction now returns
SendError::Full(value) instead of silently pushing into a queue that
has no room.

- Reserve future extracts cascade waker before dropping inner lock
- Else branch explicitly drops inner lock for symmetry
- send_with_eviction returns Full when pop_front yields None

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
